sections.name: Server
sections.text: Always on the move from patron to patron, servers are the backbone of any tavern or eatery. Their work hones both their agility and their diplomacy.

abilities.name: Ale
abilities.text: : Andoren beer, Boulderhead bock, Cheerful Delver stout, Liquid Ghosts, Luglurch ale, Thileu lager.

abilities.name: Cider and Mead
abilities.text: : Hardroot cider, Qadiran cider, Two Knight Brewery mead, Wineberry mead.

abilities.name: Wine
abilities.text: : Irrisen icewine, sarain, syrinelle, Whiterose Abbey wine

abilities.name: Spirits
abilities.text: : Daggermark drip, dragon punch whiskey, Old Erebus, vjarik.

family.name: Publicans

sections.name: Bar Fight!
sections.text: Bar fights work better without a grid. Use the stat blocks here or in the Downtrodden section for notable participants, everyone else attacking nonlethally with fists or improvised weapons. Roll one or two attacks at the end of each round against each PC in the fight (+4 attack modifier, 1d4+2 bludgeoning damage). Alcohol rules can be found here

family.text: Countless adventures begin in a tavern or a pub. Maybe it's because such places attract the risk-prone, or maybe it's because everyone needs a little liquid courage before they decide to take on a group of rampaging ogres.

traits.name: Human
traits.text: A creature with this trait is a member of the human ancestry. Humans are a diverse array of people known for their adaptability. An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by humans.

traits.name: Humanoid
traits.text: Humanoid creatures reason and act much like humans. They typically stand upright and have two arms and two legs.

automatic_abilities.effect: The server catches the dropped object before it hits the floor or leaves their reach.
automatic_abilities.name: Quick Catch
automatic_abilities.requirement: The server has at least one hand free
automatic_abilities.trigger: An object that the server could hold in one hand is dropped within the server's reach

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Nonlethal
traits.text: An effect with this trait is not inherently deadly. Damage from a nonlethal effect knocks a creature out rather than killing it. You can use a nonlethal weapon to make a lethal attack with a –2 circumstance penalty.

traits.name: Thrown
traits.text: You can throw this weapon as a ranged attack, and it is a ranged weapon when thrown. A thrown weapon adds your Strength modifier to damage just like a melee weapon does. When this trait appears on a melee weapon, it also includes the range increment. Ranged weapons with this trait use the range increment specified in the weapon’s Range entry.
